---
title: "Analyzing Music Data"
author: Isabella Hua
format: html
execute:
  echo: false
---


```{r}
#| message: false
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
billboard %>%
  select(artist, track, date.entered, wk1, wk2, wk3, wk4)
```

```{r}
# Summarize the date.entered range to show the covered time period
billboard %>%
  summarize(
    earliest = min(date.entered, na.rm = TRUE),
    latest = max(date.entered, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_records = n()
  )
```

```{r}
# Histogram of wk1 rankings with the number of values used
wk1_data <- billboard %>%
  filter(!is.na(wk1))

wk1_count <- nrow(wk1_data)

tibble(
  statistic = "n_wk1_values",
  value = wk1_count
)

ggplot(wk1_data, aes(x = wk1)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = 0.5, color = "white", fill = "#2c7fb8") +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of wk1 Rankings",
    x = "wk1 ranking",
    y = "count",
    caption = paste("Number of values used:", wk1_count)
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Histogram of wk6 rankings with the number of values used
wk6_data <- billboard %>%
  filter(!is.na(wk6))

wk6_count <- nrow(wk6_data)

tibble(
  statistic = "n_wk6_values",
  value = wk6_count
)

ggplot(wk6_data, aes(x = wk6)) +
  geom_histogram(binwidth = 1, boundary = 0.5, color = "white", fill = "#41ab5d") +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram of wk6 Rankings",
    x = "wk6 ranking",
    y = "count",
    caption = paste("Number of values used:", wk6_count)
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Compare wk1 and wk6 ranks for each song
rank_comparison <- billboard %>%
  filter(!is.na(wk1)) %>%
  mutate(
    status = case_when(
      is.na(wk6) ~ "missing_by_wk6",
      wk6 < wk1 ~ "improved",
      wk6 == wk1 ~ "same",
      wk6 > wk1 ~ "worse"
    )
  ) %>%
  count(status)

median_change <- billboard %>%
  filter(!is.na(wk1), !is.na(wk6)) %>%
  summarize(
    median_rank_change = median(wk6 - wk1)
  )

rank_comparison
median_change
```

```{r}
# Count missing and present rankings for selected week columns
selected_weeks <- c("wk1", "wk4", "wk10", "wk20", "wk40", "wk76")

billboard %>%
  select(any_of(selected_weeks)) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "week", values_to = "ranking") %>%
  mutate(status = if_else(is.na(ranking), "missing", "present")) %>%
  count(week, status) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = status, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)
```

```{r}
# Count songs by whether they re-entered the chart
billboard %>%
  select(starts_with("wk")) %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    reentered = {
      ranks <- c_across(starts_with("wk"))
      ranked_runs <- rle(!is.na(ranks))
      sum(ranked_runs$values) > 1
    }
  ) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  count(reentered)
```

```{r}
#| cache: true
# Reshape the week columns and plot each song's ranking over time
billboard_long <- billboard %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("wk"),
    names_to = "week",
    values_to = "rank"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    week = parse_number(week),
    song = paste(artist, track, sep = " - ")
  )

billboard_long %>%
  filter(!is.na(rank)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = week, y = rank, group = song)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.2, color = "steelblue") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 0.7, color = "steelblue") +
  scale_y_reverse() +
  labs(
    title = "Song rankings over time",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Rank",
    caption = "Each line represents one song's rank trajectory over weeks"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Song-level summary for first rank, best rank, first week at best rank, and total weeks on chart
song_summary <- billboard_long %>%
  arrange(artist, track, week) %>%
  group_by(artist, track) %>%
  summarize(
    first_rank = first(rank[!is.na(rank)]),
    best_rank = min(rank, na.rm = TRUE),
    first_week_best_rank = min(week[rank == min(rank, na.rm = TRUE)], na.rm = TRUE),
    total_weeks = sum(!is.na(rank)),
    reached_top10 = any(rank <= 10, na.rm = TRUE),
    reached_no1 = any(rank == 1, na.rm = TRUE),
    week_first_no1 = if (any(rank == 1, na.rm = TRUE)) min(week[rank == 1], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_integer_,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

song_summary

notable_songs <- song_summary %>%
  filter(reached_no1) %>%
  slice_min(week_first_no1, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  mutate(note = "fastest_to_number_one") %>%
  bind_rows(
    song_summary %>%
      filter(reached_no1) %>%
      slice_max(week_first_no1, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
      mutate(note = "slowest_to_number_one")
  ) %>%
  bind_rows(
    song_summary %>%
      filter(reached_top10) %>%
      slice_max(total_weeks, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
      mutate(note = "longest_top10_chart_run")
  )

notable_songs
```

```{r}
# Plot only the songs that reached the top 10, highlighting the notable songs
highlight_songs <- notable_songs %>%
  mutate(song = paste(artist, track, sep = " - ")) %>%
  select(song, note)

plot_data <- billboard_long %>%
  mutate(song = paste(artist, track, sep = " - ")) %>%
  filter(song %in% (song_summary %>% filter(reached_top10) %>% mutate(song = paste(artist, track, sep = " - ")) %>% pull(song))) %>%
  left_join(highlight_songs, by = "song") %>%
  mutate(highlight = if_else(is.na(note), "other_top10", note))

highlight_colors <- setNames(
  c("#d73027", "#4575b4", "#1a9850"),
  highlight_songs$song
)

ggplot() +
  geom_line(
    data = filter(plot_data, highlight == "other_top10"),
    aes(x = week, y = rank, group = song),
    color = "gray80",
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = filter(plot_data, highlight != "other_top10"),
    aes(x = week, y = rank, group = song, color = song),
    size = 1
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = filter(plot_data, highlight != "other_top10"),
    aes(x = week, y = rank, color = song),
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "Notable song",
    values = highlight_colors
  ) +
  scale_y_reverse() +
  labs(
    title = "Top-10 song ranking trajectories",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Rank",
    caption = "Gray lines show all top-10 songs; colored lines highlight notable songs."
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
